title: rubus erythrandrus
intro: Rubus erythrandrus, also known as the Japanese rose or wild strawberry tree, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which exhibit medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus erythrandrus include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and alleviate symptoms of allergies and respiratory diseases. Rubus erythrandrus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional East Asian medicine to treat various health conditions.
